title: Physical disc production ending in Jan 2028 for new games on PlayStation
titleZh: PlayStation 新游戏的实体光盘制作将于 2028 年 1 月结束
author: Tiberium
relevanceReason: 索尼PlayStation宣布将于2028年1月停止新游戏的实体光盘生产，转向纯数字发行，属于科技产品与数字娱乐行业重大变动。
summaryRaw: As consumer preferences and the broader entertainment industry continue to shift away from physical discs to digital, physical game disc production for all new games releasing on PlayStation consoles will be discontinued starting January 2028. Following this date, new games will be available on PlayStation Store and at retailers in digital formats only. This transition has no impact on games that already released, or will be releasing, prior to January 2028 in disc format.

This is a natural direction for Sony Interactive Entertainment to adapt to consumer trends as the general preference for digital media significantly outpaces physical discs. This transition will enable us to align more closely with how most of our community prefers to access and play games today.

We’ll continue to prioritize our resources to drive innovation in how players can access games and provide choices as to where players prefer to purchase new games, whether that’s at retailers or PlayStation Store. We remain committed to delivering a world-class gaming experience to our fans and we thank you for your continued support.
For important updates on PlayStation Store for PS3 and PS Vita announced today, please click here.
summaryZh: 索尼PlayStation宣布自2028年1月起停止新游戏的实体光盘生产，全面转向数字发行。
此举是为了顺应消费者从实体媒介向数字媒体转变的趋势。
2028年之前已发布或即将发布的实体光盘游戏不受影响。
highlightsZh: PlayStation新游戏将于2028年起仅提供数字版，实体光盘时代终结。
消费者偏好数字媒体是推动此次转型的核心原因。
索尼表示将继续优化PlayStation Store体验，为玩家提供更多购买选择。
PS3和PS Vita的PlayStation Store也有相关更新公告。
该决定反映了整个游戏行业向数字化、无实体化发展的长期趋势。

comments.author: fcsp
comments.textRawHtml:
Great buildup on Sony's side to gain trust in this move in the gaming community ahead of this announcement when just this week they again pulled hundreds of "purchased" movies from customer's libraries without refund, reminding everyone that digital content is rented, not owned.

https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2026/06/sony-erases-digital-...
comments.textZhHtml:
在宣布这一消息之前，索尼方面已经做好了充分准备，以赢得游戏界对这一举措的信任，就在本周，他们再次从客户的库中撤下了数百部“购买”的电影，且不予退款，提醒大家数字内容是租用的，而不是拥有的。

https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2026/06/sony-erases-digital-...

children.author: Marcan
children.textRawHtml:
And don't forget closing the PS3 and Vita Playstation stores, which they announced at the same time:

https://blog.playstation.com/2026/07/01/an-update-on-playsta...

This is a weird marketing strategy. They must feel free to do as they please since they know consumers are trapped.
children.textZhHtml:
别忘了关闭他们同时宣布的 PS3 和 Vita Playstation 商店：

https://blog.playstation.com/2026/07/01/an-update-on-playsta...

这是一种奇怪的营销策略。他们必须随心所欲，因为他们知道消费者被困住了。

children.author: II2II
children.textRawHtml:
> They must feel free to do as they please since they know consumers are trapped.

My take is somewhat difference: Sony is offloading the cost of their prior decisions onto consumers.

For things like movies, they should have negotiated a contract where sold copies are sold copies and cannot be revoked (even if their right to sell/rent copies lapse). For things like the PS3 store, it cannot be run indefinitely. That said, from my understanding, the authorization keys expire if the clock battery on the PS3 dies. That should not be permitted.

I don't think that this is a "do as they please" situation. I think it is a case of bad decisions being made in the past. For some, like the movies, there isn't much they can do to fix the problem after the fact. There is absolutely no incentive for the rights holders to let consumers continue to access previously purchased content (especially with Sony taking all of the blame). Even something like offering refunds to people who purchased the movies is problematic. In all probability, all of their contracts have similar terms. They would have to refund everyone for every purchase in the long run.

Other stuff, like access to PS3 purchases, are likely fixable. The question is: where is the incentive? They could create a patch for old consoles, but it would only affect a small number of customers who still have those consoles. (Worse yet, it wouldn't do anything for those who stored their consoles in the closet -- only to pull it out later to discover the authorization keys are invalid.) The math probably doesn't work out for them so they aren't going to do it.
children.textZhHtml:
> 他们必须随心所欲，因为他们知道消费者被困住了。

我的看法有些不同：索尼正在将他们之前决策的成本转嫁到消费者身上。

对于电影之类的东西，他们应该协商一份合同，其中所售副本就是已售副本且不能撤销（即使他们出售/租赁副本的权利失效）。对于像 PS3 商店这样的东西，它不能无限期地运行。也就是说，根据我的理解，如果 PS3 上的时钟电池没电，授权密钥就会过期。这是不应该被允许的。

我不认为这是一种“为所欲为”的情况。我认为这是过去做出错误决定的一个例子。对于某些人来说，就像电影中的那样，事后他们无能为力解决问题。权利持有者绝对没有动力让消费者继续访问以前购买的内容（尤其是索尼承担了所有责任）。甚至向购买电影的人提供退款之类的事情也是有问题的。他们的所有合同很可能都有相似的条款。从长远来看，他们必须向每个人的每次购买退款。

其他问题，例如购买 PS3 的权限，可能是可以修复的。问题是：动力在哪里？他们可以为旧控制台创建补丁，但这只会影响仍然拥有这些控制台的少数客户。 （更糟糕的是，对于那些将控制台存放在壁橱里的人来说，它没有任何作用 - 只是后来将其拉出来才发现授权密钥无效。）数学可能不适合他们，所以他们不会这样做。

children.author: rubyfan
children.textRawHtml:
> They would have to refund everyone for every purchase in the long run.

That is the minimum they should do. At best they should offer the movie collections for free through competitors.
children.textZhHtml:
>从长远来看，他们必须为每个购买的人退款。

这是他们应该做的最低限度。他们最多应该通过竞争对手免费提供电影收藏。

children.author: zamadatix
children.textRawHtml: There's no limit to what the best they could do is. A full refund is just what's simple, fair, and quick. I wouldn't put it past them to try to find some legal basis that the minimum is actually less than that in certain jurisdictions though.
children.textZhHtml: 他们能给出的最优方案没有上限。全额退款就是最简单、公平和快捷的方案。不过，就算他们试图寻找某些法律依据，声称在某些司法管辖区的最低赔偿标准其实低于此数，我也不会感到意外。

children.author: account42
children.textRawHtml: A forced refund to something you purchased years ago is not fair at all. At the very least they should have to pay current market price for a replacement.
children.textZhHtml: 强制退还你多年前购买的东西根本不公平。至少他们应该按当前市场价格支付替代品的费用。

children.author: II2II
children.textRawHtml:
The trouble with that is you cannot operate a business where you pay your suppliers for a product then give everything away.

I am saying that as a reflection of reality, not to absolve Sony. Someone at Sony must have understood that their licensing agreements were incompatible with the definition of sale. Someone else likely stuck some clauses into the EULA to reflect that, fully realizing that no-one reads those things (also realizing that it is not reasonable to expect consumers to read an EULA for every transaction in their life). The someone who is now responsible for executing the outcome would also understand that there is the potential for legal action on such a matter, but they also understand that there are legal machinations that will, at worse, limit the damages to a sum that is lower than the cost of refunding the full value of each and every purchase made through their service.
children.textZhHtml:
问题在于，你无法经营这样一种生意：向供应商付款购买产品，然后把一切都白送出去。

我这么说只是反映现实，并非为索尼开脱。索尼内部肯定有人明白，他们的许可协议与“销售”的定义是不相容的。另一个人可能因此在最终用户许可协议中塞入了一些条款来体现这一点，他们完全清楚没人会看这些东西（也清楚指望消费者阅读他们生活中每一笔交易的最终用户许可协议是不合理的）。如今负责执行该结果的人同样清楚，此事存在法律诉讼的风险，但他们也明白，通过法律手段运作，最坏的情况也只是将赔偿金限制在一个低于通过其服务进行的每笔购买全额退款成本的数额。

children.author: Mindwipe
children.textRawHtml:
> For things like movies, they should have negotiated a contract where sold copies are sold copies and cannot be revoked (even if their right to sell/rent copies lapse).

It's difficult to see how this could ever be possible without significant legislation changes. Nobody (but nobody) offers this, and in the vast majority of the world you literally can't buy out music rights in this way for any licensed music in the titles. That's why literally no online store offers this.
children.textZhHtml:
> 对于像电影这样的东西，他们应该协商一份合同，其中所售的副本就是出售的副本，并且不能撤销（即使他们出售/出租副本的权利失效）。

如果没有重大的立法变化，很难想象这如何可能实现。没有人（但没有人）提供此服务，并且在世界上绝大多数地区，您实际上无法以这种方式购买标题中任何许可音乐的音乐权。这就是为什么实际上没有在线商店提供此服务。

children.author: smelendez
children.textRawHtml: It works better with music because many stores will sell you a DRM-free mp3. Most movie stores are just “selling” you long-term streaming access.
children.textZhHtml: 音乐方面情况好一些，因为很多商店会卖给你无DRM的MP3。大多数电影商店只是“卖”给你长期的流媒体播放权限。

children.author: thaumasiotes
children.textRawHtml:
> For things like movies, they should have negotiated a contract where sold copies are sold copies and cannot be revoked (even if their right to sell/rent copies lapse).

This is how movies work on GOG.

In what might not be a coincidence, they haven't released any movies for many years, and the product category isn't even visible if you don't already own a movie through them.
children.textZhHtml:
> 对于像电影这样的东西，他们应该协商一份合同，其中出售的副本就是出售的副本，并且不能撤销（即使他们出售/出租副本的权利失效）。

这就是电影在 GOG 上的运作方式。

也许并非巧合，他们已经很多年没有发行任何电影了，如果你还没有通过他们拥有电影，产品类别甚至不可见。

children.author: PaulHoule
children.textRawHtml: I made a decision to get away from other consoles and only invest in Steam a while ago.  In the 2010s I was a big Vita player but Sony backed away from investing in the Vita and I saw that the kind of Japanese games I liked were coming out on Steam so I sold my Vita.
children.textZhHtml: 不久前我决定放弃其他游戏机，只投资 Steam。  在 2010 年代，我是 Vita 的忠实玩家，但索尼放弃了对 Vita 的投资，我看到我喜欢的日本游戏正在 Steam 上推出，所以我卖掉了我的 Vita。

children.author: eru
children.textRawHtml:
Steam has been (mostly) a good citizen. But there's not much in the way of legal or technical hurdles to them going down the same path. It's all goodwill and reputation.

I hope it's enough.
children.textZhHtml:
Steam（基本上）是一个好公民。 但他们走同样的道路并没有太多的法律或技术障碍。 这都是善意和声誉。

我希望这足够了。

children.author: mjevans
children.textRawHtml: If steam ever goes up in smoke, there will be infinite justification to never trust download only ever again.
children.textZhHtml: 如果Steam哪天灰飞烟灭了，那就有了无穷的理由让人再也不相信纯数字下载了。

children.author: fluoridation
children.textRawHtml: You can at least make local backups of your Steam games. If a game is removed such that you can't even download it anymore, you can always just install it manually.
children.textZhHtml: 你至少可以对Steam游戏进行本地备份。如果某款游戏被下架，导致你连下载都下载不了了，你总归可以手动安装。

children.author: j1436go
children.textRawHtml: That's what GOG offers. And I'd advise anyone to use GOG over Steam for their principles  alone. Every game you purchase through their platform is DRM free and they go to a lot of effort to preserve retro titles that would be difficult to play nowadays without a lot of tinkering. They also put no limitation on how many family members you can share your games with.
children.textZhHtml: 这正是GOG提供的服务。我建议大家优先使用GOG而不是Steam，单凭他们的理念就足够了。你在他们平台上购买的每一款游戏都是无DRM的，而且他们付出了大量努力来保存那些复古游戏，如果没有这些努力，现在玩这些老游戏需要大费周章。他们对你可以与多少家庭成员分享游戏也不设任何限制。

children.author: dtech
children.textRawHtml: Steam has DRM,so no. Easily cracked yes, but then you can just go the full pirate route.
children.textZhHtml: Steam带有DRM，所以不行。虽然很容易破解，但那样你干脆就彻底走盗版路线了。

children.author: fluoridation
children.textRawHtml: The point is that with Steam you have more options than with consoles.
children.textZhHtml: 关键在于，相比主机，Steam能提供更多选择。

children.author: lordfrikk
children.textRawHtml: Not completely true, many games on Steam in fact do not have any DRM. See https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48755132.
children.textZhHtml: 这并不完全准确，Steam上其实有很多游戏根本没有DRM。请参阅https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48755132。

children.author: Hikikomori
children.textRawHtml: Optional DRM, but essentially on by default. Could maybe consider it malicious compliance as its so easy to bypass but still allows them to check the DRM checkbox.
children.textZhHtml: DRM是可选的，但实际上默认开启。这或许可以算作一种恶意合规，因为它极易被绕过，却仍能让他们勾选DRM选项。
